methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $260M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 7.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Chroma Medicine is a Series B biotech company founded by Angus McQuilken and Vic Myer, having raised $260M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws from a comparable set of 8 private-stage peers in Biotech & Health alongside 3 public market anchors (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $260M total raised proxy, implying a valuation of $494M. Peer-median analysis references five same-sector, same-stage companies—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—yielding a median implied valuation of $810M and a range of $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring is inapplicable due to lack of target revenue disclosure, though the Biotech & Health public median trades at 7.4x P/S.

The two applicable methods triangulate to a valuation range of $420M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $652M. The wide dispersion reflects meaningful uncertainty around Chroma's relative positioning within the peer cohort. Stage-progression yields the more conservative estimate, while peer-median reflects current market conditions for comparably-staged biotech assets. Key uncertainties include Chroma's clinical progress, pipeline composition, and capital efficiency relative to named peers.
